homepage.heroSubline: Serving DFW homes with expert electrical solutions since 2012. Trusted by thousands for safe, efficient, and lasting repairs.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Apex Electrical has grown from a small family-owned business into one of Dallas' most trusted electrical contractors. We've worked on everything from historic Oak Cliff homes to sleek Uptown condos.
Our approach blends old-school craftsmanship with modern safety standards. Every job begins with a thorough inspection and ends with a meticulous cleanup—because your home deserves nothing less.
We’re proud to support local Little Leagues and the Dallas Arts District. When you hire us, you’re investing in a team that cares deeply about this community.

valueProps.title: Same-Day Availability
valueProps.description: We prioritize emergency calls, especially during North Texas storm season when downed wires and outages strike.

valueProps.title: Upfront Pricing
valueProps.description: No surprises—just honest quotes that account for Dallas' unique housing challenges like aging wiring in Lake Highlands.

valueProps.title: DFW-Trained Technicians
valueProps.description: Our team understands local codes for both historic Bishop Arts bungalows and new Frisco developments.

valueProps.title: 100% Satisfaction Guarantee
valueProps.description: If you're not happy, we’ll make it right—no arguments.

homepage.ctaSubline: Call 555-ELE-R92 for immediate service across Dallas-Fort Worth.

services.name: Emergency Electrical Repairs
services.shortDescription: 24/7 solutions for sparks, outages, and dangerous wiring issues.
services.description:
When your lights flicker during a Plano thunderstorm or an outlet stops working in your Preston Hollow home, you need help fast. We arrive within 90 minutes with fully stocked trucks to diagnose and fix:

Common emergencies include tripped breakers in older McKinney homes, buzzing panels in Arlington ranches, and frayed wires in Dallas’ historic properties. Our team carries specialized tools to handle aluminum wiring common in 1970s builds.

Safety first: We’ll secure the hazard area, explain the problem in plain English, and provide temporary power solutions if needed. Before leaving, we test all nearby circuits to prevent collateral damage.
process: Step 1: Immediate phone assessment to determine risk level
Step 2: On-site diagnosis with thermal imaging cameras
Step 3: Repair using materials rated for Texas heat
Step 4: Post-repair safety inspection
benefits: Available during North Texas’ worst storms
No overtime charges for nights/weekends
Complimentary whole-home surge protector quote
Follow-up within 24 hours

faq.question: What constitutes an electrical emergency?
faq.answer: Any situation risking fire or shock—sparking outlets, burning smells, or complete loss of power in Dallas’ summer heat require immediate attention.

faq.question: Do you service older homes with knob-and-tube wiring?
faq.answer: Yes, we safely update antique systems in areas like Winnetka Heights, often adding GFCI protection without compromising historic charm.

services.name: Electrical Panel Upgrades
services.shortDescription: Modernize your home’s power center for safety and capacity.
services.description:
Dallas homes built before 1990 often have 60-amp panels that can’t handle modern appliances. We specialize in upgrading to 200-amp service without disrupting your daily life.

In Frisco’s newer homes, we install smart panels that let you monitor usage via phone. For historic Tudors in East Dallas, we conceal new panels in closets or basements to preserve aesthetics.

The process includes load calculations specific to Texas summers (when AC units run constantly), NEC-compliant breakers, and whole-home surge protection. We handle all permits for Collin County and Dallas County.
process: Step 1: Evaluate current panel and home energy needs
Step 2: Obtain necessary city permits
Step 3: Install new panel with minimal drywall damage
Step 4: Test all circuits and update your home’s electrical map
benefits: Prevents overheating in attics (common in Texas ranch homes)
Eliminates the need for dangerous double-tapped breakers
Prepares your home for EV chargers and solar panels
10-year warranty on labor

faq.question: How long does a panel upgrade take?
faq.answer: Most Dallas homes are completed in 6-8 hours. Historic homes with plaster walls may require extra time for careful retrofitting.

faq.question: Will my power be off during the upgrade?
faq.answer: We coordinate with Oncor to minimize downtime, typically keeping outages under 2 hours during the swap.

services.name: Ceiling Fan Installation
services.shortDescription: Energy-efficient cooling tailored to your room’s size and style.
services.description:
Properly installed ceiling fans can cut AC costs by 40% in Dallas’ scorching summers. We assess your space to recommend optimal blade span (52” for most living rooms, 44” for bedrooms).

For vaulted ceilings in Plano McMansions, we use extended downrods. In low-ceilinged Oak Lawn condos, we install hugger models. Every fan gets balanced with laser tools to prevent the wobble common in cheap DIY jobs.

We retrofit existing light fixtures and reinforce boxes to handle fan weight—especially important in 1950s homes where original boxes weren’t designed for modern fixtures.
process: Step 1: Assess ceiling structure and wiring
Step 2: Install reinforced mounting bracket
Step 3: Wire fan with remote/hardware per your preference
Step 4: Balance blades and test all functions
benefits: Reduces reliance on overworked AC units
Includes decorative light kit installation
Quieter operation than big-box store specials
Optional smart home integration

faq.question: Can you install fans on angled ceilings?
faq.answer: Yes, we adapt mounts for cathedral ceilings common in Frisco’s newer builds, ensuring proper stability.

faq.question: Do you haul away old fans?
faq.answer: We responsibly dispose of old units and can donate working models to Dallas Habitat for Humanity.

services.name: Outdoor Lighting
services.shortDescription: Enhance safety and curb appeal with weatherproof illumination.
services.description:
Texas’ extreme weather demands durable lighting. We install path lights that withstand 100°F summers and occasional ice storms, using commercial-grade copper wiring to prevent corrosion.

For Uptown townhomes, we design subtle uplighting to highlight architecture. In sprawling Allen backyards, we create layered lighting zones with motion sensors. All installations include GFCI protection—critical for Dallas’ heavy rains.

Popular projects include pergola string lights in Bishop Arts, security floods for Preston Hollow estates, and low-voltage garden lighting at White Rock Lake homes.
process: Step 1: Nighttime walkthrough to identify dark zones
Step 2: Select fixtures rated for North Texas UV exposure
Step 3: Bury wiring 18” deep to avoid lawn equipment damage
Step 4: Program smart controls (if applicable)
benefits: Deters burglars—a concern in Dallas’ growing areas
Extends outdoor living time during mild Texas evenings
LED options last 5x longer than incandescent
No disruptive trenching for low-voltage systems

faq.question: Can lighting survive Dallas hail storms?
faq.answer: We use polycarbonate lenses and aircraft-grade aluminum housings that have withstood 2” hail in field tests.

faq.question: How much does landscape lighting cost?
faq.answer: A basic 6-fixture path lighting system starts at $1,200 installed. Larger properties with smart controls average $3,500-5,000.

services.name: Generator Installation
services.shortDescription: Keep power flowing during Texas’ frequent outages.
services.description:
When DFW’s grid fails during ice storms or summer demand peaks, a standby generator keeps your family safe. We install 10kW-22kW units sized for your home’s essential circuits.

For McKinney’s historic districts, we conceal generators with custom fencing. In new Frisco developments, we integrate with smart home systems. Every install includes:

- Automatic transfer switch that activates in seconds
- Natural gas or propane hookups (we coordinate with Atmos Energy)
- Permitted enclosures meeting Dallas noise ordinances
- Load testing to simulate outage conditions
process: Step 1: Home energy audit to determine generator size
Step 2: Obtain permits and utility approvals
Step 3: Pour concrete pad (if needed)
Step 4: Install and test under load
benefits: Protects perishables and medical equipment
Maintains AC/heat during extreme weather
Increases home value (especially in outage-prone areas)
5-year service included

faq.question: How loud are standby generators?
faq.answer: Our Kohler units operate at 68 dB—quieter than a vacuum—and we position them away from bedrooms per Dallas code.

faq.question: Can I install a generator myself?
faq.answer: Texas law requires licensed electricians for permanent installations due to gas line and transfer switch hazards.

reviews.name: Martha R.
reviews.text: When our Lakewood bungalow’s bathroom outlet started smoking during a storm, Apex had a technician here in 45 minutes. He found corroded wiring from a 1950s DIY job and replaced it with waterproof GFCI outlets—even sealed the exterior penetration where moisture was getting in.

reviews.name: James T.
reviews.text: Our 1978 Richardson home kept tripping breakers every summer. Apex upgraded us to 200-amp service and added surge protection. When the next big storm hit, our neighbors lost power for hours while our lights stayed on. Worth every penny.

reviews.name: Lisa M.
reviews.text: They installed 5 Hunter fans in our new Preston Hollow build—the one in the game room is huge but doesn’t wobble at all. The electrician took time to explain the different speed settings and even programmed the remotes to match our existing smart home system.

reviews.name: Carlos S.
reviews.text: Apex transformed our dark Oak Lawn backyard into an evening oasis. The path lights have survived two Texas hailstorms intact, and the motion-activated floods make my wife feel safer when she comes home late from her hospital shifts.

reviews.name: Sarah K.
reviews.text: After being without power for 3 days during the 2023 ice storm, we had Apex install a 22kW Generac. It kicked on automatically during last month’s outage—ran our whole house including both AC units. The installer’s attention to detail on the gas line connection was impressive.

reviews.name: Darnell W.
reviews.text: Bought a 1920s craftsman in Winnetka Heights with scary knob-and-tube wiring. Apex’s team rewired the entire house without damaging the original plaster. They even replicated antique push-button switches in the parlour where we wanted to keep the historic look.

serviceAreas.content:
Apex Electrical proudly serves the Fort Worth community with expert electrical services tailored to its unique needs. The city's charming Craftsman bungalows near downtown often require specialized electrical upgrades to preserve their historic character while meeting modern demands. Our team is skilled in handling the challenges posed by Fort Worth's hot, humid summers, ensuring your home's electrical systems remain safe and efficient year-round. We also provide comprehensive electrical solutions for newer developments, blending reliability with the latest technology.

From panel upgrades to whole-home rewiring, Apex Electrical helps Fort Worth homeowners maintain safe and functional electrical systems. Whether you're restoring a historic property or upgrading a modern home, our licensed electricians deliver precision workmanship with a focus on safety and compliance. We understand the importance of preserving Fort Worth's architectural heritage while implementing energy-efficient solutions for today's living needs.
serviceAreas.localContext: Fort Worth features Craftsman bungalows near downtown and experiences hot, humid summers that stress electrical systems.

serviceAreas.content:
Apex Electrical is your trusted partner for electrical services in Plano's well-established neighborhoods. The city's 1980s-90s subdivisions often require electrical updates to accommodate today's power demands for smart homes and modern appliances. Our team specializes in upgrading older electrical systems while maintaining the integrity of these classic homes. We also help Plano residents prepare their electrical systems for the intense Texas summers when air conditioning units run continuously.

From ceiling fan installations to complete home rewiring, our licensed electricians provide reliable service for Plano homeowners. We understand the specific electrical needs of mature subdivisions, including outdated wiring, insufficient outlets, and aging electrical panels. Apex Electrical offers energy-efficient solutions that enhance safety while reducing utility costs in these established communities.
